preflight_quotes.discoveryUnlocks.js: When a player discovers a world-map location (nodeId), the wiring in questSystem.initDiscoveryQuestBridge() looks up the locationId here and calls startQuest() for any non-pending entry.
preflight_quotes.regions.js: Region unlock gates through the milestone engine via dependencies. Arizona is always available as the starting region.
preflight_quotes.worldMapData.js: Quest for Glory-style world expansion. Each location is an optional sub-scene that offers unique materials, knowledge, and side quests without blocking main progression.
preflight_quotes.runtimeAudit.js: discoveryUnlocks.js:14-21 documents that its keys are WORLD_LOCATIONS IDs (from worldMapData.js), NOT regions.js IDs. Validating against regions.js alone is therefore too coarse and produces false-positive errors for every valid location-level key (desert_foraging, copper_mine, salt_river, mountain_range).
preflight_quotes.quests.js: No matches found for desert_foraging, copper_mine, salt_river, or mountain_range — none of the four flagged keys are referenced in any quest definition.
preflight_quotes.swarm-orchestrator.md: Never edit code yourself. You orchestrate. Workers code.

notes.investigation_summary: Dispatch reported four runtime audit errors. Audit code (runtimeAudit.js lines 220-236) builds validKeys = regions.js REGIONS array (ids: arizona, andes, arabian, turkish, persian, swahili, chinese, pakistan, malay) UNION Object.keys(WORLD_LOCATIONS) from worldMapData.js (desert_foraging, copper_mine, salt_river, dry_wash). Three of four flagged keys (desert_foraging, copper_mine, salt_river) ARE WORLD_LOCATIONS keys — they pass the audit as of commit e7419a3. Only mountain_range is genuinely failing: it is a key in worldMapData.REGIONS (placeholder for Superstition Mountains) but is NOT a WORLD_LOCATIONS key and is NOT in regions.js. The dispatch's claim of four errors appears stale — at most one error remains.

per_key_action.desert_foraging: no change — already passes audit (matches WORLD_LOCATIONS.desert_foraging)
per_key_action.copper_mine: no change — already passes audit (matches WORLD_LOCATIONS.copper_mine)
per_key_action.salt_river: no change — already passes audit (matches WORLD_LOCATIONS.salt_river)
per_key_action.mountain_range: removed — placeholder region (worldMapData.REGIONS.mountain_range) with no matching WORLD_LOCATIONS entry; the discovery event fires per-location, so a region-level key cannot fire. No quest dependency (was pending:true, questId:null).

per_key_quest_dependency.desert_foraging: none in quests.js (used in scenes/DesertForagingScene.js getLocationId() and sideQuestSystem.js side-quest location tags only)
per_key_quest_dependency.copper_mine: none in quests.js (used in scenes/CopperMineScene.js getLocationId() and sideQuestSystem.js side-quest location tags only)
per_key_quest_dependency.salt_river: none in quests.js (used in scenes/SaltRiverScene.js getLocationId() and sideQuestSystem.js side-quest location tags only)
per_key_quest_dependency.mountain_range: none in quests.js (used only as worldMapData.REGIONS placeholder + WorldMapScene.js WORLD_REGION_BIOME mapping for terrain rendering — both unaffected by removing the discoveryUnlocks entry)

notes.audit_logic_rationale: The audit at runtimeAudit.js:236 correctly unions regions.js region ids and WORLD_LOCATIONS keys. The mountain_range failure is a real data drift — the discoveryUnlocks entry uses a worldMapData REGIONS key (region-level) while the audit only accepts regions.js REGIONS or WORLD_LOCATIONS (location-level). Since per-location discovery is the correct granularity for the bridge wiring, removing the entry is the right fix.
